titleStmt.title: Letter from Marie Martin to her mother Mary Martin, 27 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Marie Martin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Marie Martin (1892-1975) to her mother Mary Martin (née Moore). In this letter Marie is anxious to hear news from home as several members of the family have mumps. Marie Helena Martin (1892-1975) later known as 'Mother Mary', was a volunteer nurse during the First World War. She served in field hospitals in Malta, Leeds, and France working for the British army and later went on to become the founder of the Medical Missionaries of Mary (MMM).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
27.8.16 My very dearest mother, Ever so many
thanks for your letters. I am
very distressed to hear of
all your worry & trouble
with the mumps etc. but
was delighted to receive
your card saying they were
all better. When you are so
busy & worried just get
someone to drop me a card
to say how you all are.
It is too bad about Jack
I cannot quite understand
how his temperature goes up
he really does not seem
very strong. I shall write
you a long letter in my
off time to-day but want
this to catch the post
It must be a great upset
to Ethel but a great use
to you to have her at
home. Have you had another
cold, do take care of your-
self. Hoping you continue
to have good news of
Tommy.
My fondest love in
great haste
Marie
